content.problemStatement:
Given two words AAA, BBB and a set WWW consisting of MMM distinct words W1,W2,…,WMW_1, W_2, \dots, W_MW1​,W2​,…,WM​. Every word has same length and consists of lowercase english characters.

A **transformation sequence** from word AAA to word BBB using a set of words WWW is a sequence of words A→S1→S2→⋯→Sl→BA \rightarrow S_1 \rightarrow S_2 \rightarrow \dots \rightarrow S_l \rightarrow BA→S1​→S2​→⋯→Sl​→B such that,


Every adjacent pair of words differs by a single letter.
Every SiS_iSi​ for 1≤i≤l1 \leq i \leq l1≤i≤l should be in WWW. Note that AAA, BBB does not need to be in WWW.
Find the number of words in the **shortest transformation sequence** from AAA to BBB, or −1-1−1 if no such sequence present.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated words AAA, BBB.
Then next line each test case contains integer MMM — number of distinct words in WWW.
The next line contain MMM space-separated words WiW_iWi​ — the ithi^{th}ith word.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of words in the **shortest transformation sequence** from AAA to BBB, or −1-1−1 if no such sequence present.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
chef chef
2
chef chaf
abc acb
5
abd acd acj aab cbb
a j
2
c q
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: As AAA, BBB both same words so the shortest transformation sequence will have one word chef only.
Test case 222: The shortest transformation sequence will be abc →\rightarrow→ abd →\rightarrow→ acd →\rightarrow→ acb. So there will be 444 words in the sequence.
Test case 333: The shortest transformation sequence will be a →\rightarrow→ j.

hidden.input:
3
chef chef
2
chef chaf
abc acb
5
abd acd acj aab cbb
a j
2
c q
